role_summary: Civil Rights History Project interviewee; the LoC oral history is the primary archival source for this catalog page.
ai_reading: Gwendolyn Annette Perkins Duncan's neighbor counts are moderate (peak 94), with Robert Bagner Hayling (entry 103) as the top match and the broader cluster including Barbara Edna Vickers (#5), Purcell Maurice Conway (#99), Audrey Nell Hamilton and JoeAnn Anderson Ulmer (#4). The catalog flags a +0.89 reading toward Armed Self-Defense on the Nonviolence as Theology vs Armed Self-Defense axis and a -0.89 reading toward Tactical Pragmatism on the Tactical Pragmatism vs Strategic Vision axis as the most-distinctive structural reading.
biographical_paragraph: Gwendolyn Annette Perkins Duncan's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 51 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. The LoC transcript carries a publication-block audit tier: known issues exist that prevent full publication-grade certification, and any specific biographical or quoted claims drawn from the transcript itself should be confirmed against external sources before citation. Independent biographical sourcing for an extended orientation paragraph is pending; the strongest signal currently available is the embedding pattern named in the AI's reading above.
